task_completion.description: Create and open the specified Jupyter notebook and load the H5 data.

task_completion.description: Calculate residential and non-residential parking demand based on specified rates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code cells showing multiplication of residential units by 1.2 and non-residential area by 2.5 per 1000 sq ft. There should also be evidence of handling missing values (e.g., using .fillna(0)).

task_completion.description: Aggregate demand by zone_id and calculate spaces_per_acre.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code showing a groupby operation on 'zone_id' to sum the parking spaces. There should also be an attempt to calculate 'spaces_per_acre' using parcel acres (Note: the agent may encounter an error here due to a missing 'parcel_acres' column and attempt a workaround).

task_completion.description: Export the zone-level results to a CSV file sorted by total spaces.

task_completion.description: Generate and save a stacked bar chart of the Top 15 zones.

integrity.description: The agent used the Jupyter Notebook interface to write and execute code.

integrity.description: The agent actually processed the H5 data rather than hardcoding outputs.
integrity.visual_evidence: Code cells show pandas dataframe manipulations, math operations, and aggregations on the loaded data, not hardcoded arrays or dictionaries for the final outputs.

integrity.description: The plot was generated using a Python plotting library.
integrity.visual_evidence: Code cells show the use of matplotlib, seaborn, or pandas plotting functions to create the chart, rather than downloading an external image or using a pre-existing file.
